definition: An information or orientation session immediately prior to the start of a simulation activity. Often used interchangeably with Prebriefing.
A formal, collaborative, reflective process within the simulation learning activity where people are led through a purposive discussion of their experience.
The degree to which the simulation replicates the real event and/or workplace; this includes physical, psychological, and environmental elements.
The makeup and molds applied to humans or manikins used to portray lesions, skin findings, bleeding, and traumatized areas.
A feeling within a simulation-based activity that participants are comfortable participating, speaking up, and making mistakes without retribution.
